beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Nathan wakes at home in Wallingford; yesterday's energy was 35/100 (post-adventure fatigue), so a quiet morning routine fits. Drizzle outside means no immediate outdoor plans.

beats.activity: Commute to Interbay office in light rain.
beats.reasoning: Steady rain 7am–2pm; Nathan drives from Wallingford to Interbay (westbound, unaffected by SR-520 eastbound closure). Budget 40 min for wet roads and rain.

beats.activity: Morning spreadsheet work; tax-season accounting tasks.
beats.reasoning: It's Tuesday during tax season; Nathan is meticulous with spreadsheets and settles in for focused morning work despite yesterday's adventure. Rain outside keeps him office-bound.

beats.activity: Lunch: quick salad and sandwich at desk or nearby café.
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady at 12:30; Nathan stays local, eats light, and keeps the day low-key. Yesterday's legs-still-buzzing energy suggests he's pacing himself.

beats.activity: Afternoon accounting work; tax filings and client reconciliation.
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by 2pm; Nathan is still in work mode and doesn't ditch early today (yesterday he did). Afternoon push to close out spreadsheets.

beats.activity: Drive home from Interbay to Wallingford.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute; rain has cleared. Budget 45 min for typical Tuesday traffic heading northeast from Interbay.

beats.activity: Short trail run at nearby Green Lake or local neighborhood loop.
beats.reasoning: Rain cleared by afternoon; Nathan is a trail runner and hiker. Legs are still buzzing from yesterday, so a shorter, easy 45-min evening loop to shake out fatigue and clear his head.

beats.activity: Shower, dinner prep, and cooking at home.
beats.reasoning: Back at home after the run. Nathan is self-reliant and meticulous; he cooks dinner at home rather than going out, keeping the evening quiet and budget-conscious.

beats.activity: Dinner, review expense spreadsheets, listen to podcast on porch.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down mirroring yesterday's tone: quiet, introspective, spreadsheet-focused. Nathan is a night owl, so he stays up until 22:30 before winding down for bed.

diary: Woke to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle and just knew it was going to be one of those days. The commute wasn't too bad—I'd checked the bridge closure alerts before leaving, so I took I-5 instead of dealing with the SR-520 mess. The office was its usual tax-season chaos: spreadsheets, client reconciliations, the whole nine yards. Grabbed a sad desk salad around one and powered through until nearly 5:30. The drive home felt longer than usual, probably because my legs were already itching to move. Hit Green Lake for a quick loop once the rain finally stopped—nothing fancy, just needed to shake off the fluorescent office feeling. Cooked some simple pasta, showered, and settled on the porch with a podcast. The evening cooled down nicely. Felt good to end the day moving, even if just a little.
thought_process: Tax season has me locked into that desk-bound rhythm, so I've learned to protect my evenings fiercely. The rain and bridge closure didn't really derail me—I check traffic alerts religiously during spring, it's just part of the routine now. I chose the Green Lake run because I needed physical reset more than anything else; after seven hours hunched over numbers, even twenty minutes of movement makes the whole day feel less suffocating. The podcast on the porch was deliberate too—a way to decompress without scrolling my phone. I'm aware I'm in that grinding phase, and I compensate by being intentional about small escapes. Not hiking yet this season, but these neighborhood runs keep me sane until tax season ends and I can get to the real mountains again.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
